purpose: Versioned, evidence-reviewed source registry compiled into INSIGHT's private-estate runtime. Road-only, historic-area and non-estate management records remain here for audit context but are not installed as estates.
accuracyDefinition: No known false positives or false negatives within a versioned, manually reviewed fixture set; unresolved properties remain unclassified. This is not proof of exhaustive Surrey coverage, title ownership or legal-boundary exactness.
sourceLimitations: HM Land Registry Price Paid Data does not contain a private-estate classification.
HM Land Registry title plans normally show general rather than exact legal boundaries.
Unadopted, privately maintained, privately owned, gated, historic and resident-managed are not interchangeable concepts.
Marketing descriptions are discovery evidence only and cannot activate a classification without corroboration.
A map navigation anchor locates an estate for interaction; it is not a legal centroid or boundary.

classifications.B: Single private road or small road-management entity
classifications.C: Historic estate, character area or multi-association private-road network
classifications.D: Modern gated, managed or design-led residential development
classifications.E: Country or landed estate rather than a resident estate

productCohorts.notes: Product cohort lists the identities approved for the current product edition. Runtime activation additionally requires a ready or partial_ready install status; research classification and road governance do not activate an entity. A residents company or private road cannot create an estate entity by itself.

installStatuses.ready: Evidence supports encoding and testing; approved-cohort records with this status compile into the installed runtime
installStatuses.partial_ready: The verified core may be encoded and tested while every listed fringe or partial-road rule remains unclassified
installStatuses.hold: Do not classify until the listed blocker is resolved
installStatuses.reclassify: Retain only in a non-estate contextual category
installStatuses.exclude: Exclude from the private residential estate registry

classifierRequirements: Use one canonical registry for collection, display, filtering and Ask INSIGHT.
Parse and match structured address fields; never use unrestricted substring matching.
Apply explicit property exclusions before inclusions.
Use exact canonical street plus locality and postcode for whole-road rules.
Use house number, UPRN or an authoritative polygon for partial roads.
Recompute every transaction from an empty derived estate field.
Persist rule version, matched rule id, evidence status and review status on every classified row.
Use the registry labelPoint for estate pins; never replace it with the median of matched transactions.
Keep displayBoundary and classificationGeometry separate and provenance-controlled.
Held entities must produce zero automatic classifications.

currentRuleRemovals.reason: Matches unrelated addresses such as Wentworth Court in Egham.
Matches unrelated addresses such as St George's Place in Esher.
Marketing text/name collision and roads outside the verified operational core.
Use the complete 17-road schedule; Friars Close is not on the official map.
Section One is the evidenced Icklingham Road segment; Fairmile Avenue belongs to the wider Eaton Park Estate and the other similarly named roads remain outside both rules.
Use the official six-road estate plot map and partial Ashley Park Avenue rule.

remove: Separate EATON PARK ROAD estate entity
currentRuleRemovals.reason: Separate road governance is metadata, not a second estate. Eaton Park Road, Fairmile Avenue, The Drive and Fairmeads are constituent roads of the wider Eaton Park Estate.

remove: Separate EWELL DOWNS ROAD and THE GREEN estate entities
currentRuleRemovals.reason: Council evidence describes the two streets as forming part of one private estate.
Private road, residents association and private estate are not interchangeable product entities.
Several separate road-management and frontage associations exist.
Clare Hill appears to sit within a wider private golf-course estate with several roads; the umbrella member/title schedule is required.
Esher Park Avenue, New Road and Meadway require separate entities and schedules.
It is a historic neighbourhood, not one governed private estate.

acceptanceTests: Wentworth Court, Ridgemead Road, Egham must remain unclassified.
St George's Place, Esher must remain unclassified.
Goldrings Road must not classify as Crown Estate.
Friars Close must not classify as Burwood Park.
Ashley Rise and 197 Ashley Park Avenue must not classify as Ashley Park.
Fairmile Avenue KT11 2JA/2JB must classify as Eaton Park; Fairmile Lane and Fairmile Park must not.
Eaton Park Road, The Drive and Fairmeads must classify as Eaton Park and must never create separate estate entities.
Ewell Downs Road and The Green in Ewell must both classify as Ewell Downs Road & The Green.
GreenMeads must appear once as the estate containing Meads Road and Green Lane.
Blackdown Avenue, Redcourt and Virginia Beeches must not appear in the private-estate runtime.
Held entities must return no automatic match.
Every activated rule must have positive, adjacent-road negative and name-collision negative fixtures.
Changed historical rows must be exported and reviewed by estate, road and postcode before release.
